name: Any Time Bus Co Inc
recent_news: No notable web presence or news found; website (anytimebus.com) was unreachable during research.
fit_notes: Hauppauge/Long Island NY school bus operator with ~169 employees and ~$9.5M est. revenue per source data. Minimal online footprint suggests a small family-owned operator; could be a tuck-in candidate in a dense Suffolk County market, but identity and operations need primary-source verification before outreach.

name: Krise Bus Svc
owner_or_parent: American Student Transportation Partners (ASTP), owned by CVC DIF (acquired from Access Holdings Aug 2025); founded by Tim Krise (2016)
recent_news: ASTP (Krise's parent) sold by Access Holdings to CVC DIF in Aug 2025. Krise absorbed Brandywine Transportation's 300+ routes/vehicles in 2023, expanded to 6 new districts for 2022-23 school year.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target — Krise is the flagship PA platform of ASTP, a competing PE-backed roll-up now owned by CVC DIF. Better viewed as a direct competitor to Summit for PA acquisitions; potentially a competitor-bid scenario or future platform-trade candidate, not a near-term family-owned tuck-in.

name: Ramp Truck & Bus Ctr
districts_or_customers: Port Jefferson area (Suffolk County) — specific contracts not confirmed
recent_news: No recent M&A or material news identified. Company has been operating ~50+ years, raising natural succession considerations.
fit_notes: Long-tenured (founded 1973) Port Jefferson Station NY operator with 158 employees and ~$7.6M est. revenue. Some sources classify it as automotive/vehicle-related rather than purely school bus, so service mix needs primary verification. If a legitimate school bus operator, the 50+ year history and independent ownership make it an attractive Suffolk County tuck-in with possible succession dynamics.

name: L & M Bus Co
owner_or_parent: First Student, Inc. (acquired parent Total Transportation Corp. on June 30, 2022)
principals: Agostino Vona (historically listed as L&M owner)
recent_news: L&M is a subsidiary of Total Transportation Corp., which was acquired by First Student on June 30, 2022. TTC serves 100,000+ NY-metro students across 15 locations and ~3,200 buses.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target — already owned by First Student (EQT Infrastructure portfolio) via the 2022 TTC acquisition. Brooklyn-based NYC DOE contract holder is part of a major strategic competitor. Useful only as competitive intel on First Student's NY-metro footprint.

name: Matthews Bus Co Inc Sch Buses
owner_or_parent: Summit School Services LLC (I Squared Capital portfolio — Summit's own brand; previously National Express/NELLC)
recent_news: Transitioned from National Express LLC (NELLC) to Summit School Services as part of the broader National Express North American school bus carve-out acquired by I Squared Capital.
fit_notes: ALREADY OWNED by Summit School Services — should be removed from the tuck-in target list. McKees Rocks PA operation is part of Summit's existing platform footprint; data point useful for adjacency mapping (find neighbors of this terminal as tuck-ins).
